description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three irregularly shaped, vertically oriented rocks with a rough, weathered surface texture. The rocks are closely grouped, with the tallest one positioned at the back and the shortest at the front, creating a natural, uneven silhouette. The surface is predominantly gray with subtle variations in shading, suggesting the presence of fine cracks and natural stone grain. The edges are angular but not sharp, and the overall form is blocky, resembling granite or similar hard stone. There are no visible artificial markings, carvings, or signs of human modification. The object appears solid and heavy, suitable for use as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of three rough, gray, vertically oriented rocks with a weathered, natural stone texture and blocky, irregular shapes.
description_view_0: Two main rocks are visible, with the tallest on the left and a slightly shorter one to the right, both showing rough, cracked surfaces.
description_view_1: The tallest rock is centered, with the other two rocks partially visible behind, highlighting the angular, blocky forms.
description_view_2: All three rocks are visible, with the shortest in the foreground and the tallest in the back, emphasizing the cluster arrangement.
description_view_3: The tallest rock dominates the view, with only a small portion of the shortest rock visible to the left, showing the largest continuous stone surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
